import numpy as np
import torch
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings\('ignore'\)
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors
import umap

from core import generate_sparse_data, run_experiment_multi_seed, generate_correlated_features, device

S = 0.95
n_viz = 2000

# Generate data
datasets = {}
for n in [5, 16]:
    torch.manual_seed\(42\)
    datasets[n] = generate_sparse_data\(n_viz, n, S\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)

# ========== SURPRISE 1: Data structure analysis ==========
print\("=" * 60\)
print\("SURPRISE 1: Why AE beats t-SNE/UMAP at neighborhoods"\)
print\("=" * 60\)

for n in [5, 16]:
    x = datasets[n]
    n_active = \(x > 0\).sum\(axis=1\)
    print\(f"\\nn={n}: distribution of # active features:"\)
    for k in range\(min\(n+1, 6\)\):
        frac = \(n_active == k\).mean\(\)
        print\(f"  {k} active: {frac:.3f} \({\(n_active == k\).sum\(\)} samples\)"\)
    print\(f"  mean: {n_active.mean\(\):.2f}"\)
    on_axis = \(n_active <= 1\).mean\(\)
    print\(f"  0-1 active: {on_axis:.3f}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python 2>&1 <<'PYEOF'
import numpy as np
import torch
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from core import generate_sparse_data, run_experiment_multi_seed, device

torch.manual_seed\(42\)
x = generate_sparse_data\(2000, 16, 0.95\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)

# PCA
pca = PCA\(n_components=2\)
z_pca = pca.fit_transform\(x\)

# AE
res = run_experiment_multi_seed\(n=16, m=2, l=1, S=0.95, n_seeds=10, n_steps=10000,
                                importance_decay=0.7, verbose=False\)
model = res['model']
model.eval\(\)
with torch.no_grad\(\):
    z_ae = model.encode\(torch.tensor\(x, dtype=torch.float32, device=device\)\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)

fig, axes = plt.subplots\(1, 2, figsize=\(14, 6\)\)
c = \(x > 0\).sum\(axis=1\)

for ax, z, title in [\(axes[0], z_ae, 'Autoencoder'\), \(axes[1], z_pca, 'PCA'\)]:
    ax.scatter\(z[:, 0], z[:, 1], c=c, cmap='viridis', s=8, alpha=0.6\)
    ax.set_title\(title, fontsize=14\)
    ax.set_aspect\('equal'\)
    ax.grid\(True, alpha=0.2\)

plt.suptitle\('n=16, m=2, l=1 — closer look', fontsize=16\)
plt.tight_layout\(\)
plt.savefig\('/tmp/ae_vs_pca_n16.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight'\)
plt.close\(\)

# Now check: what are the PCA components doing?
print\("PCA components \(2x16\):"\)
print\(pca.components_\)
print\(f"\\nExplained variance ratio: {pca.explained_variance_ratio_}"\)
print\(f"Total explained: {pca.explained_variance_ratio_.sum\(\):.4f}"\)

# How many features have substantial weight on each PC?
for i in range\(2\):
    weights = np.abs\(pca.components_[i]\)
    print\(f"\\nPC{i+1} weights: {pca.components_[i].round\(3\)}"\)
    print\(f"  # features with |weight| > 0.1: {\(weights > 0.1\).sum\(\)}"\)

# And the AE encoder weights
W_ae = model.encoder.weight.detach\(\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)
print\(f"\\nAE encoder weight W \(2x16\):"\)
print\(W_ae.round\(3\)\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python 2>&1 <<'PYEOF'
import numpy as np
import torch
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from core import generate_sparse_data

# Check per-feature variance in the data
torch.manual_seed\(42\)
x = generate_sparse_data\(2000, 16, 0.95\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)

print\("Per-feature variance \(all should be ~equal since data generation has no importance weighting\):"\)
variances = x.var\(axis=0\)
print\(np.round\(variances, 5\)\)
print\(f"\\nMax/min variance ratio: {variances.max\(\)/variances.min\(\):.2f}"\)

print\(f"\\nPCA explained variance ratio: only captures {PCA\(n_components=2\).fit\(x\).explained_variance_ratio_.sum\(\):.1%} of total variance"\)
print\("\(With 16 equal-variance independent features, 2 PCs can capture at most ~2/16 = 12.5%\)"\)

# In the Toy Models paper, the DATA has importance weighting \(I_i * x_i\),
# so high-importance features have higher variance and PCA would pick them out.
# Here, importance is only in the LOSS, not the data.
print\("\\n--- What if we importance-weight the DATA like Toy Models paper? ---"\)
importance = np.array\([0.7**i for i in range\(16\)]\)
x_weighted = x * importance[None, :]
variances_w = x_weighted.var\(axis=0\)
print\(f"Weighted per-feature variance: {np.round\(variances_w, 5\)}"\)
print\(f"Max/min ratio: {variances_w.max\(\)/variances_w[variances_w > 0].min\(\):.1f}"\)

pca_w = PCA\(n_components=2\)
pca_w.fit\(x_weighted\)
print\(f"\\nWeighted PCA components:"\)
for i in range\(2\):
    top = np.argsort\(np.abs\(pca_w.components_[i]\)\)[::-1][:3]
    print\(f"  PC{i+1}: top features = {top}, weights = {pca_w.components_[i][top].round\(3\)}"\)
    print\(f"        explained var = {pca_w.explained_variance_ratio_[i]:.4f}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(python 2>&1 <<'PYEOF'
import numpy as np
import torch
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.manifold import TSNE
import umap
from core import generate_sparse_data

torch.manual_seed\(42\)
x = generate_sparse_data\(2000, 16, 0.95\).cpu\(\).numpy\(\)

# Run t-SNE and UMAP, then color by which feature is active
tsne_z = TSNE\(n_components=2, perplexity=30, random_state=42\).fit_transform\(x\)
umap_z = umap.UMAP\(n_components=2, random_state=42\).fit_transform\(x\)

n_active = \(x > 0\).sum\(axis=1\)

fig, axes = plt.subplots\(2, 2, figsize=\(16, 14\)\)

for col, \(z, method\) in enumerate\([\(tsne_z, 't-SNE'\), \(umap_z, 'UMAP'\)]\):
    # Top row: color by which single feature is active \(gray for multi/zero\)
    ax = axes[0, col]
    # Zero vectors
    zero_mask = n_active == 0
    ax.scatter\(z[zero_mask, 0], z[zero_mask, 1], c='lightgray', s=5, alpha=0.3, label=f'zero \({zero_mask.sum\(\)}\)'\)
    
    # Multi-feature
    multi_mask = n_active >= 2
    ax.scatter\(z[multi_mask, 0], z[multi_mask, 1], c='black', s=8, alpha=0.5, label=f'multi \({multi_mask.sum\(\)}\)'\)
    
    # Single-feature, colored by which feature
    colors = plt.cm.tab20\(np.linspace\(0, 1, 16\)\)
    single_mask = n_active == 1
    for fi in range\(16\):
        feat_mask = single_mask & \(x[:, fi] > 0\)
        if feat_mask.sum\(\) > 0:
            ax.scatter\(z[feat_mask, 0], z[feat_mask, 1], c=[colors[fi]], s=15, alpha=0.8,
                      label=f'f{fi} \({feat_mask.sum\(\)}\)'\)
    
    ax.set_title\(f'{method}: colored by active feature', fontsize=13\)
    ax.legend\(fontsize=5, ncol=3, loc='upper left', bbox_to_anchor=\(1.01, 1\)\)
    
    # Bottom row: color by magnitude \(for single-feature samples only\)
    ax = axes[1, col]
    ax.scatter\(z[zero_mask, 0], z[zero_mask, 1], c='lightgray', s=5, alpha=0.3\)
    ax.scatter\(z[multi_mask, 0], z[multi_mask, 1], c='black', s=8, alpha=0.3\)
    
    for fi in range\(min\(8, 16\)\):  # top 8 features
        feat_mask = single_mask & \(x[:, fi] > 0\)
        if feat_mask.sum\(\) > 0:
            sc = ax.scatter\(z[feat_mask, 0], z[feat_mask, 1], c=x[feat_mask, fi], 
                          cmap='plasma', s=15, alpha=0.8, vmin=0, vmax=1\)
    
    ax.set_title\(f'{method}: single-feature samples colored by magnitude', fontsize=13\)
    plt.colorbar\(sc, ax=ax, label='feature value', shrink=0.7\)

plt.tight_layout\(\)
plt.savefig\('/tmp/tsne_umap_anatomy.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight'\)
plt.close\(\)
print\("Saved"\)

# Count: where do the zero vectors end up relative to single-feature points?
print\("\\n=== Zero vector placement ==="\)
print\(f"Zero vectors: {zero_mask.sum\(\)}, single-feature: {single_mask.sum\(\)}, multi: {multi_mask.sum\(\)}"\)
print\(f"\\nt-SNE zero vector spread \(std\): {tsne_z[zero_mask].std\(axis=0\)}"\)
print\(f"t-SNE single-feat spread \(std\): {tsne_z[single_mask].std\(axis=0\)}"\)
print\(f"t-SNE overall spread \(std\):     {tsne_z.std\(axis=0\)}"\)
print\(f"\\nUMAP zero vector spread \(std\):  {umap_z[zero_mask].std\(axis=0\)}"\)
print\(f"UMAP single-feat spread \(std\):  {umap_z[single_mask].std\(axis=0\)}"\)
print\(f"UMAP overall spread \(std\):      {umap_z.std\(axis=0\)}"\)
PYEOF)
